title: Build OVO 2D Platformer with Levels and Portals

requirements.summary: Implement core 2D platformer mechanics in useOvoGame.ts including smooth running, jumping, wall-jumping, sliding, coyote time, and gravity-based physics for the player character.
acceptanceCriteria: Player can run left and right with smooth acceleration and deceleration
Player can jump with realistic gravity arc
Wall-jump and slide mechanics are functional
Coyote time allows brief jump window after walking off a platform edge

file_operations.description: Enhance player physics in the game loop to add smooth acceleration/deceleration for horizontal movement, wall-jump detection and mechanics (detect wall contact, allow jump with horizontal velocity boost away from wall), wall-slide friction when touching a wall while falling, and coyote time implementation (track frames since leaving ground, allow jump input within coyote window). Update the player state to include velocity components, grounded state, wall-contact state, and coyote timer. Ensure gravity is applied each frame with a realistic arc.

requirements.summary: Design and implement at least 10 platformer levels in useOvoGame.ts with increasing difficulty, varied platform layouts, hazards, gaps, and moving platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 10 distinct levels are defined
Each level has a unique platform layout
Difficulty visibly increases across levels (wider gaps, more hazards, more complex paths)
Levels load correctly in sequence

file_operations.description: Expand the levels array to include at least 10 distinct level definitions. Each level should specify unique platform coordinates, hazard positions (spikes, pits), gap widths, and optionally moving platform definitions with their movement patterns. Ensure difficulty progression by increasing gap distances, adding more hazards, introducing tighter timing windows, and more complex vertical navigation in later levels. Add logic to load and transition between levels in sequence.

requirements.summary: Implement portal objects in useOvoGame.ts that, when the player touches them, teleport the player to a specified target position within the same level or to the start of a specified level.
acceptanceCriteria: Portal objects are placed in level definitions with a target position or target level
Player is instantly teleported when touching a portal
Portals are visually distinct (e.g., glowing ring or swirl animation) on the canvas
Portals can target a different coordinate in the same level or jump to a new level

file_operations.description: Add a portal array to each level definition, where each portal specifies its position (x, y), size, and target (either a destination coordinate within the current level or a target level index). In the game loop collision detection, check if the player overlaps any portal hitbox. On collision, immediately update the player position to the portal's target coordinates, or transition to the target level if specified. Store portal animation state (e.g., rotation angle, pulse phase) for rendering.

requirements.summary: Implement a level exit/end goal in each level in useOvoGame.ts. When the player reaches the exit, they progress to the next level. After the final level, the game is considered complete and the score (time elapsed) is submitted.
acceptanceCriteria: Each level has a clearly marked exit object
Touching the exit transitions the player to the next level
Completing the final level triggers the game-complete state
A timer tracks elapsed time as the score

file_operations.description: Add an exit object to each level definition with position and hitbox dimensions. In the game loop, check for collision between the player and the exit. On collision, increment the current level index and reset player position to the start of the next level. If the current level is the final level (index equals levels.length - 1), set a gameComplete flag to true. Track elapsed time in milliseconds from game start, updating each frame, and expose this as the final score.

requirements.summary: Update OvoGame.tsx to render portals on the canvas with a visually distinctive animated appearance (e.g., pulsing glow or swirl), display the current level number, and show a game-complete screen with score submission and leaderboard when all levels are finished.
acceptanceCriteria: Portals are drawn with an animated visual effect on the canvas each frame
Current level number is displayed in the HUD
Game-complete overlay appears after the final level with elapsed time score
Score submission and leaderboard components are shown on completion

file_operations.description: In the canvas rendering loop, add drawing logic for portals using the portal data from useOvoGame. Draw each portal as a circle or ring with a gradient fill, applying an animated pulsing effect (scale or opacity oscillation) and rotation based on the portal animation state. Update the HUD rendering to display the current level number (e.g., 'Level X/10'). Add conditional rendering outside the canvas for a game-complete overlay that appears when gameComplete is true, showing the elapsed time score converted to seconds, and including the ScoreSubmission and Leaderboard components as already implemented for other games.
